title: The Stag at the Pool
lines: A Stag came to a clear pool to drink and saw his reflection in the water.
He looked with great pleasure at his magnificent horns.
"What splendid antlers I have," he said proudly.
But when he looked at his legs in the reflection, he was ashamed.
"How thin and weak they are!" he cried.
"They are not worthy of such fine horns."
Just then the sound of hunting dogs came from the forest.
The Stag sprang away with great speed on his thin legs.
He easily escaped the dogs and reached the forest.
There his magnificent horns became caught in a tree, and he was trapped.
"My thin legs, which I despised, would have saved me; but my fine horns have caused my ruin."
moral: We often despise what is truly valuable.
